behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real git rename detection plus CLI parsing/output. A unit test of classification logic can pass while real `git diff --name-status`/rename-threshold behavior causes misclassification. REAL-INFRA applies (real repo, filesystem, git subprocess output).
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI as a subprocess, and assert report shows exactly one rename entry and zero add/delete entries for that path.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Integration because behavior depends on real git subprocess rename semantics; mocked/stubbed diff output can hide the failure.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily about real binary diff/subprocess/output encoding behavior. Mocks usually provide clean text and miss decode/crash paths triggered by actual git output and binary blobs.
behaviors.test_description: Create a repo with a committed binary file change, invoke the CLI subprocess, assert zero crash/exit failure and verify binary file is included in the reported counts.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Integration because fake diff payloads do not reliably reproduce real binary/subprocess edge cases.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The critical failure mode is handling real git behavior in an unborn-HEAD repository (exit codes/messages). That boundary is subprocess+repo state, not isolated function logic.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, run the CLI subprocess, assert successful exit and an empty structured report.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Integration because the behavior depends on real git no-commit semantics and subprocess errors.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is end-to-end correctness against actual repository history. Real commits/index/worktree interactions and git query behavior must be exercised; in-process fakes can drift from true git semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo with a controlled sequence of adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run CLI subprocess, and assert category totals and file lists match the known history.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Integration because behavior explicitly requires fidelity to actual repository history, which is a real git/system boundary.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged semantics are defined by git index/worktree state and command behavior. Real repo/index transitions are needed to catch mismatches; mocked state often masks these differences.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create equivalent change sets once as staged and once as unstaged, run CLI subprocess for each scenario, and assert the structured outputs are identical.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Rejected Integration because correctness hinges on real git index/worktree behavior and subprocess interaction.
